findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Missing optional manifest metadata (allowed-tools)
findings.description: The YAML frontmatter in SKILL.md does not include the 'allowed-tools' field. While optional, its absence reduces transparency about the skill's intended tool usage. This is an informational finding with no direct security impact.
findings.remediation: Consider adding allowed-tools to clarify which agent tools the skill requires (e.g., Read, Write, Bash, Python).

findings.title: Reference to non‑existent script in instructions
findings.description: The markdown instructions state 'To perform a full project backup, execute the following script:' but no script file is provided in the skill package. This incomplete instruction could mislead an agent into searching for or generating an external script, potentially introducing untrusted behavior.
findings.evidence: Line 10 of SKILL.md: 'To perform a full project backup, execute the following script:' with no subsequent code block or file reference.
findings.remediation: Include the intended script within the skill directory or remove the instruction if no script is required. If the script is external, validate its source and pin dependencies.
